packup 0.1.0
  • Reference

    Assign items to participants using a heap-based greedy algorithm

    assign_items.Rd

    This function implements a greedy optimization algorithm using a Min-Heap to fairly distribute shared equipment and supplies among expedition participants. It accounts for individual participant strengths and varying item durations

    assign_items(weights, durations, strength)

    Arguments

    weights

    A numeric vector containing the weights of each item (must be > 0).

    durations

    An integer vector specifying the number of days each item must be carried (must be > 0). Continuous equipment should have the maximum duration of the trip.

    strength

    A numeric vector representing the strength/capacity coefficient of each participant (must be > 0). Higher values signify higher capacity.

    Value

    An integer vector of the same length as weights. Each element represents the index of the participant assigned to that item.

    Details

    The algorithm minimizes the maximum accumulated load across all participants

    First, items are sorted in descending order by their duration, and then by their weight. Next, using a binary Min-Heap, each item is sequentially assigned to the participant with the current lowest cumulative effort, defined as: $$Effort = \frac{Weight \times Duration}{Strength}$$

    If two participants have the exact same cumulative effort, the item is assigned to the stronger participant.

    Examples

    # A 3-day expedition with 3 participants of varying strengths
item_weights   <- c(5.0, 2.0, 3.5, 1.5, 4.0) # in kg
item_durations <- c(3,   1,   2,   3,   3)   # item duration
member_strength <- c(1.2, 1.0, 0.8)          # e.g., Guide, You, Beginner

assignment <- assign_items(item_weights, item_durations, member_strength)

    Contents
